summary: Frontend-only regression test for Phase 2 of YOLO26 CV platform. All 13 requested test cases PASS. Landing page renders Claude light theme (Pampas #F4F3EE body background confirmed), GitHub stars button and sidebar GitHub link both point to https://github.com/dyglo/deplyze-vision. All nav buttons (launch-studio-btn, hero-benchmark-btn, 5x task-pill-*) route correctly with query params. Studio page compiles cleanly (no webpack error overlay), compare-mode toggle shows second canvas with data-testid=compare-canvas, task switching across Detect/Pose/Segment/Classify/Track works without crash. Benchmark page shows 'No Benchmark Data Yet' empty state, 3 tabs (Overview, Per-Task Metrics, Compare Runs) present, Open Studio CTA navigates to /studio. All 6 sidebar nav links (Studio/Benchmark/Projects/Datasets/Model Hub/Results) route correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Studio.jsx is ~720 lines — consider splitting compare-mode logic and result-table rendering into smaller components for maintainability (advisory, not blocking).

TF.js WebGL backend init fails in headless/preview env (no GPU). This is an environmental limitation (not a code bug); models would lazy-load to CPU/WASM in such cases. Ensure a fallback backend message is shown in UI if WebGL keeps failing — currently a warning only in console.

Canvas2D 'willReadFrequently' warning observed on nav to Studio — add {willReadFrequently: true} when calling getContext('2d') on canvases that repeatedly use getImageData for slight perf improvement.

success_rate.backend: N/A (backend not in scope for this iteration)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 feature set verified: Claude theme tokens applied correctly (body bg = rgb(244,243,238) / Pampas), Crail #C15F3C accent visible on Detect pill and logo, GitHub links use https://github.com/dyglo/deplyze-vision everywhere, compare mode renders dual-canvas grid-cols-2 with compare-canvas testid, task switch from detect→pose→segment→classify→track does not crash (clearInput/clearResult runs). Benchmark empty state works. No webpack overlay, no pageerror. The prior webpack JSX issue main agent mentioned (CompareIcon hoist fix at line 350) is confirmed working. TF.js WebGL is unavailable in the headless browser used for testing (expected); do NOT treat that console warning as a bug.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA — all requested Phase 2 features verified working end-to-end. The only console warnings were: (1) TF.js WebGL backend not supported in headless browser (env limitation, falls back automatically), (2) Canvas2D willReadFrequently hint (perf advisory only).
